data.body:
G3 container coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 8937 "Producer<->consumer snapshot contract: shared schema + round-trip test through production path"), re-cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57).

This child is the keystone and FULLY covers its owned criteria: parent AC1 (SSOT snapshot-entry schema; producer output + consumer reads validate) → 8937 AC1+AC2; parent AC2 (one round-trip contract fetcher→snapshot→differs→applier through the production path, real-payload fixtures) → 8937 AC3 (fixtures delegated to fe57, depends_on declared); parent SC1 (no silent producer/consumer shape drift) → satisfied by the joint round-trip, reinforced by fe57 + 59ef. Contributes to SC5/AC7 via 8937 AC4 (semantic assertions; no whole-blob golden equality / no mock-call-count). Criteria not owned are correctly delegated: AC3/SC2→33d0; AC4/SC3→2359; AC5/SC4→822a; fixtures/fake→fe57; verified-fake honesty→59ef. No per-criterion gap attributable to child 8937 itself.

The single roster-level AC6 absence (documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNING new tests) is already recorded on this ticket and across siblings, with 8937 named as its natural home — emitted as the structured finding below for this pairing's record; not a coverage gap of 8937's owned criteria.
